name: Tamil Nadu
stopoverNote: Chennai (MAA) is the main international gateway, while Madurai (IXM) is convenient for flying out after the south-bound circuit. The Chennai–Madurai rail corridor is excellent — book Pandian, Vaigai or Shatabdi-class trains on IRCTC well ahead.

diet.vegetarian: Tamil Nadu is one of India's easiest states for vegetarian travel. Banana-leaf meals, idli, dosa, vada, pongal, upma, sambar and chutneys are ubiquitous; temple towns and classic tiffin restaurants are usually pure vegetarian.
diet.vegan: Idli, plain dosa, rava dosa, sambar, rasam, coconut chutney and many rice dishes are naturally vegan, but ask for no ghee or butter in pongal, masala dosa fillings and temple-style tempering. Filter coffee uses cow's milk, so ask for black coffee or carry plant milk.

cities.name: Chennai
cities.notes: Tamil Nadu's coastal capital — British-era Fort St. George, Mylapore temples, Marina Beach, Chola bronzes and deep filter-coffee culture.

cities.name: Mahabalipuram
cities.notes: Mamallapuram — a 7th-century UNESCO coastal village of Pallava rock-cut temples, epic bas-reliefs, monolithic rathas and sculpture workshops.

cities.name: Pondicherry
cities.notes: Puducherry — French colonial villas, Tamil quarters, Sri Aurobindo Ashram, Auroville, cafés and Coromandel beaches, seamlessly paired with the Tamil Nadu coast.

cities.name: Thanjavur
cities.notes: Tanjore — Chola civilisation's heart, with the soaring Brihadeeswarar Temple, palace museums, bronze icons, Tanjore paintings and Carnatic heritage.

cities.name: Madurai
cities.notes: Tamil Nadu's spiritual capital — Meenakshi Amman Temple, Nayak palace architecture, flower markets, jasmine, Jigarthanda and 2,500 years of Tamil urban life.

itinerary.sim: Any Indian prepaid SIM (Jio or Airtel) works well; buy at Chennai airport or an official store with photo ID. Coverage is strong across the Chennai–Madurai corridor, with only occasional patchiness on rural ECR and delta roads.

Chennai.name: Chennai
Chennai.note: Two days covers the Mylapore/colonial core, Marina Beach and the Egmore bronze collections; keep starts early to beat heat and humidity.

days.theme: Mylapore temples, colonial fort & Marina Beach
days.note: Start Kapaleeshwarar by 7am, then use metro or autos between Mylapore, Fort St. George and Marina; Fort Museum closes Fridays.

activities.name: Kapaleeshwarar Temple, Mylapore
activities.tip: Arrive 6:30–8am for morning rituals; photograph the gopuram from the tank side and dress modestly.

activities.name: Mylapore lanes & San Thome Basilica
activities.tip: The neo-Gothic basilica is built over the tomb of St Thomas; the crypt museum holds ancient relics.

activities.name: Fort St. George & Museum
activities.tip: Carry photo ID for security; St Mary's Church inside is India's oldest Anglican church.

activities.name: Marina Beach sunset walk & street food
activities.tip: Do not swim because of rip currents; try sundal and bhajji stalls for ₹20–₹30.

days.theme: Egmore bronzes, Triplicane & T. Nagar shopping
days.note: Museums are clustered in Egmore; this is an easy half-day before shopping or departing for Mahabalipuram the next morning.

activities.name: Government Museum, Egmore — Bronze Gallery
activities.tip: The Chola bronze collection is a perfect primer before Thanjavur; photography costs extra.

activities.name: Parthasarathy Temple, Triplicane
activities.tip: One of India's oldest Vaishnava temples and usually quieter than Kapaleeshwarar.

activities.name: T. Nagar Pondy Bazaar & Ranganathan Street
activities.tip: Use Nalli Silks or Co-optex for more reliable fixed-price silk shopping.

activities.name: South Indian breakfast tiffin crawl
activities.tip: Order idli, dosa, vada, sambar and filter coffee; vegan travellers should skip coffee or ask for black.

meals: Murugan Idli Shop idli and podi

Mahabalipuram.name: Mahabalipuram
Mahabalipuram.note: One early-start day is enough for the UNESCO monument cluster; add a night only if you want beach time or sculpture-workshop browsing.

days.note: All ASI monuments sit within a compact 2km zone; start at 7am, finish core sights by early afternoon, then use the beach road for food and workshops.

activities.name: Arjuna's Penance / Descent of the Ganges bas-relief
activities.cost: ASI combo ticket ₹40 (Indian) / ₹600 (foreign); children under 15 free
activities.tip: Best light is 7–8am; look for the cat doing penance and the elephant family.

activities.name: Shore Temple at sunrise
activities.tip: The Bay of Bengal setting is the whole point; rough NE monsoon seas can disrupt the shoreline.

activities.name: Pancha Rathas monolithic temples
activities.tip: Each single-rock ratha prototypes a later South Indian temple form; don't miss the elephant and lion sculptures.

activities.name: Krishna's Butter Ball and cave temples
activities.tip: Mahishasuramardini and Varaha caves have excellent Pallava carvings within a short walk.

activities.name: Beach road sculpture workshops
activities.tip: Watch granite carvers work with traditional chisels before an easy beachside meal.

Pondicherry.name: Pondicherry
Pondicherry.note: Two relaxed days suit White Town, the Ashram, cafés, Auroville and beaches; rent a bicycle or scooter for short hops.

days.note: White Town is compact and walkable; morning light is best on colonial façades, while the Ashram requires silence and modest clothing.

activities.name: Sri Aurobindo Ashram
activities.tip: Open roughly 8am–12pm and 2pm–6pm; silence and no photography around the samadhi.

activities.name: White Town heritage walk — Rue de la Marine, Romain Rolland Street, Goubert Avenue
activities.tip: Go 7–9am for pastel villas, bougainvillea, the French War Memorial and quiet lanes.

activities.name: Promenade / Goubert Avenue beachfront
activities.tip: It is a rocky seafront, not a swimming beach; sunrise and evening strolls are best.

activities.name: White Town café breakfast
activities.tip: Baker Street, Café des Arts and La Maison Rose are popular; vegan options are usually fruit bowls, toast and black coffee.

activities.name: Manakula Vinayagar Temple and Tamil Quarter
activities.tip: A lively Ganesha temple that contrasts beautifully with the French colonial quarter.

meals: Café des Arts or La Maison Rose

days.note: Auroville is 10–12km from White Town; outer Matrimandir viewing is easy, while the inner chamber requires advance booking.

activities.name: Matrimandir viewing point, Auroville
activities.cost: Free; inner meditation chamber free with advance pass
activities.tip: Book the inner chamber 2–3 days ahead; the golden sphere and gardens are extraordinary even from the viewing point.

activities.name: Auroville Visitors Centre, shops & organic café
activities.tip: Good place for Auroville history, handmade goods and vegan-friendly organic food.

activities.name: Serenity Beach
activities.tip: A real sandy beach with caution-needed swimming and occasional surf-board rentals.

activities.name: Paradise Beach by boat from Chunnambar
activities.tip: A 15-minute boat ride reaches a cleaner, quieter family-friendly beach south of town.

Thanjavur.name: Thanjavur
Thanjavur.note: Two days allow the Big Temple in morning and evening light plus the palace bronzes and nearby Chola temple circuit.

days.note: The Big Temple rewards lingering; visit at dawn for puja and again at golden hour when the granite glows.

activities.name: Brihadeeswarar Temple morning puja & Nandi Hall
activities.tip: Arrive 6:15–6:30am for conch, lamps and the colossal single-stone Nandi.

activities.name: UNESCO complex inscriptions, murals and vimana
activities.tip: The 66m granite vimana and 80-tonne capstone are Chola engineering marvels; a licensed guide adds depth.

activities.name: Evening Sayarakshai puja and golden-hour return
activities.tip: Return around 6pm for nadaswaram, tavil and amber stone light with fewer crowds.

activities.name: Tanjore painting and music-craft shopping
activities.tip: Look for workshops near South Main Street or fixed-price Poompuhar-style government emporia.

hotels.name: Mid-range AC hotel near Big Temple

days.theme: Royal Palace, Saraswathi Mahal & nearby Chola temples
days.note: Palace and library are adjacent; use the afternoon for Darasuram or a wider Kumbakonam temple detour.

activities.name: Thanjavur Maratha Royal Palace & Art Gallery
activities.tip: The Chola bronze gallery, Darbar Hall and Bell Tower are the key stops; reconfirm current counter fees.

activities.name: Saraswathi Mahal Library Museum
activities.tip: Rare palm-leaf manuscripts and illustrated medical and literary texts are the highlights.

activities.name: Airavatesvara Temple, Darasuram (UNESCO)
activities.tip: The intimate Chola site is famous for stone chariot wheels, musical pillars and fine miniature carvings.

activities.name: Kumbakonam temple detour
activities.tip: Add Adi Kumbeswarar, Sarangapani and Swamimalai brass-casting if you want deeper temple craft context.

hotels.name: Mid-range AC hotel near Big Temple

Madurai.name: Madurai
Madurai.note: Two days gives Meenakshi in both morning and evening ritual modes, then palace, Gandhi Museum and market life without rushing.

days.theme: Meenakshi Amman Temple — the living world wonder
days.note: Visit early for puja atmosphere and again for the night ritual; use a licensed guide if you want to decode the mandapams.

activities.name: Meenakshi Amman Temple main complex
activities.cost: Free general darshan; special darshan ₹50 per deity / ₹100 both; camera ₹50
activities.tip: Enter via the East Tower; Hall of 1000 Pillars and Golden Lotus Tank are essential.

activities.name: Palliyarai night ritual at Alankara Mandapam
activities.tip: Arrive by 8:45pm for the 9:15pm palanquin procession with music, lamps and chanting.

activities.name: Puthu Mandapam market
activities.tip: The 16th-century mandapam is packed with silk, religious items, jasmine and temple colour.

activities.name: Madurai tiffin and Jigarthanda stop
activities.tip: Murugan Idli is the classic pure-veg option; Jigarthanda is milk-based and not vegan.

hotels.name: Mid-range AC hotel near railway station

days.note: The palace opens at 9am and the Gandhi Museum is nearby; markets are best early morning for flowers or late afternoon for snacks.

activities.name: Tirumalai Nayakkar Mahal
activities.tip: The 17th-century palace has 18m-high pillars; evening sound-and-light shows cost extra.

activities.name: Gandhi Museum, Madurai
activities.tip: A moving independence-movement museum in Tamukkam Palace, including the blood-stained dhoti exhibit.

activities.name: Madurai flower, banana and Netaji Road bazaars
activities.cost: Free to walk; fruit and flowers ₹20–₹200
activities.tip: The flower market is most intense 4–7am; later, chase peanuts and street snacks near Netaji Road.

activities.name: Thiruparankundram Murugan Temple
activities.tip: One of the six abodes of Lord Murugan, partly carved into a hillside 9km from Madurai.

hotels.name: Mid-range AC hotel near railway station

meals: Konar Kadai area veg tiffin options

connections.method: Road by bus or hired cab via East Coast Road / Old Mahabalipuram Road
Road by East Coast Road — the scenic coastal drive
Road by hired cab or train via Villupuram Junction
Train via Trichy or road by taxi/bus

extras: The classic route is Chennai → Mahabalipuram → Pondicherry → Thanjavur → Madurai, tracing the Coromandel Coast before turning inland through the Chola heartland.
November–February is the most comfortable window for plains, coast and temples; April–June is very hot, while October–December can bring north-east monsoon disruption on the coast.
Mahabalipuram's ASI combo ticket covers Shore Temple, Pancha Rathas, Arjuna's Penance and the cave-temple zone; quoted Indian/foreign fees are as of 2026 and should be reconfirmed on-site.
A cab hired from Chennai for the ECR day can cover Mahabalipuram and continue to Pondicherry, often making better use of the coastal drive than separate transfers.
Pondicherry is a Union Territory, not Tamil Nadu administratively, but it is geographically embedded in the coast and is part of this practical circuit.
Ooty is best treated as a separate Nilgiri extension of 2–3 nights via Coimbatore; it breaks the south-bound temple-and-coast route logic.
